title: linaria latifolia
intro: Linaria latifolia, commonly known as the Large Toadflax or Great Yellow Toadflax, is a perennial plant species belonging to the figwort family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides. The plant is also valued for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Linaria latifolia is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat respiratory conditions and promote overall well-being.
